channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: What we know about the Baltimore bridge collapse
item.description: A cargo ship rammed into Baltimore’s Francis Scott Key Bridge, causing the span to collapse and presumably killing six construction workers.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and JAMES POLLARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Republican states file lawsuit challenging Biden's student loan repayment plan
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — A group of Republican-led states is suing the Biden administration to block a new student loan repayment plan that provides a faster path to cancellation and lower monthly payments for millions of borrowers.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sen. Bob Menendez decides not to delay May trial with appeal of judge's ruling
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New Jersey Sen. Bob Menendez will not appeal a judge’s ruling on Constitutional grounds that would have delayed his May trial, his lawyers said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Gridlock expected as Biden, Obama and Clinton arrive in NYC for fundraiser
item.description: President Joe Biden and former Presidents Barack Obama and Bill Clinton will be in New York City Thursday for a fundraiser.
item.dc:creator: Julie Millet, NewsNation

item.title: Florida man ‘sick of caring’ for friend’s cats accused of killing felines: authorities
item.description: A Florida man faces multiple counts of cruelty to animals after surrendering seven cats to the SPCA, five of which were dead at the time.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Migrants becoming more aggressive at US border: Memo
item.description: Texas law enforcement officials wrote in a memo to Border Patrol agents that they are fearful for their safety.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Illinois stabbing spree suspect charged with murder after rampage leaves four dead, seven injured
item.description: Christian Soto, 22, was identified as the suspect who allegedly stabbed people in Rockford, Illinois, on Wednesday, killing four and injuring seven others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden administration restores threatened species protections dropped by Trump
item.description: BILLINGS, Mont. (AP) — The Biden administration on Thursday restored rules to protect imperiled plants and animals that had been rolled back under former President Donald Trump. Among the changes, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's team cites First Amendment in contesting charges in Georgia election interference case
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — The charges against Donald Trump in the Georgia election interference case seek to criminalize political speech and advocacy conduct that the First Amendment protects, a lawyer for the former president said Thursday as he argued that the indictment should be dismissed.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Green Day will headline United Nations-backed global climate concert in San Francisco
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Green Day will headline a United Nations Human Rights-backed global climate concert on Tuesday at the famed Fillmore in San Francisco. The intimate event, which is co-hosted by the Recording Academy, aims to bring attention to the inequalities exacerbated by climate change.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Man accused of assaulting NYC women, sounding alarm on TikTok arrested
item.description: A Brooklyn man was arrested for allegedly punched a woman on the street, after a string of similar incidents led to women sounding the alarm on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sea turtle with missing flipper shows off recovery progress
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — A sea turtle with a missing front flipper is showing off their recovery progress after it was accidentally caught by a fisherman near Myrtle Beach several months ago. According to the South Carolina Aquarium, Ricotta the green sea turtle was taken in on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Woman sexually assaulted on flight home from Taylor Swift concert files lawsuit
item.description: A woman who was sexually assaulted on a Delta Airlines flight on her way home from attending a Taylor Swift concert has filed a lawsuit against the airline and her attacker. The assault happened on March 20, 2023, on a flight from Phoenix to Seattle.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Uber reveals Beyoncé partnership ahead of 'Cowboy Carter' release
item.description: Giddyup! It seems as if everyone's jumping on the "Cowboy Carter" carriage, even Uber. The rideshare app finally revealed its collaboration with the global superstar.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: ICE arrests 216 illegal migrants with cocaine, fentanyl and heroin convictions
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) has arrested 216 illegal immigrants who have convictions for drug trafficking or multiple drug possessions, all of whom are expected to be deported.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Maryland seeks $60M in federal help to start recovery plan for collapsed Baltimore bridge
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Massive barges carrying cranes streamed toward Baltimore on Thursday to begin the challenging work of removing twisted metal and concrete in an attempt to open a key shipping route blocked by the wreckage of the Francis Scott Key Bridge. Maryland Gov.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Diddy, Shyne cover-up: Woman says Diddy shot her in face in 90s
item.description: Natania Reuben says Sean "Diddy" Combs shot her in the face during the infamous Club New York shooting in 1999.
item.dc:creator: Devan Markham, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sun's corona will be visible during total solar eclipse: How to see it
item.description: If there weren't already enough reasons to travel to an area along the path of totality during the solar eclipse, here's another unique viewing opportunity.
item.dc:creator: Nick DeGray, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: VP Harris says US agencies must show their AI tools aren't harming people's safety or rights
item.description: U.S. federal agencies must show that their artificial intelligence tools aren't harming the public, or stop using them, under new rules unveiled by the White House on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Suspect charged with murder, attempted murder in a series of stabbings in Rockford, Illinois
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — A 22-year-old man has been charged with four counts of first-degree murder in a frenzied stabbing rampage that killed four people in a matter of minutes in a northern Illinois city, authorities said Thursday. Winnebago County State’s Attorney J.
item.dc:creator: KATHLEEN FOODY, RICK CALLAHAN and COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fallen crypto mogul Sam Bankman-Fried sentenced to 25 years
item.description: Bankman-Fried, 32, was convicted in November of fraud and conspiracy — a dramatic fall from a crest of success that included a Super Bowl advertisement and celebrity endorsements.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Fallen crypto mogul Sam Bankman-Fried sentenced to 25 years in prison
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Crypto entrepreneur Sam Bankman-Fried was sentenced Thursday to 25 years in prison for a massive fraud that unraveled with the collapse of FTX, once one of the world’s most popular platforms for exchanging digital currency.
item.dc:creator: KEN SWEET and LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: US changes how it categorizes people by race and ethnicity. It's the first revision in 27 years
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — For the first time in 27 years, the U.S. government is changing how it categorizes people by race and ethnicity, an effort that federal officials believe will more accurately count residents who identify as Hispanic and of Middle Eastern and North African heritage.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Man injured in kiosk fire near Lincoln Memorial
item.description: A man was injured after a kiosk near the Lincoln Memorial in Washington, D.C., caught fire Thursday morning.
item.dc:creator: Gabby Allen, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Federal EV charging stations are key to Biden's climate agenda, yet only 4 states have them
item.description: Electric vehicle charging ports are a key part of President Joe Biden’s effort to encourage drivers to move away from gasoline-powered cars and trucks that contribute to global warming, but only four states have them.
item.dc:creator: Alexa St. John, Matthew Daly And Joshua A. Bickel, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Private jet nearly crashes into Tennessee airport
item.description: According to Tullahoma Municipal Airport Manager Jon Glass, a private company jet crashed into the fence between the airfield and the terminal building Wednesday morning, stopping shockingly close to the building.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCullough, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Amid wars and White House race, celebs weigh risks of speaking out
item.description: Celebrities went all in during the 2020 and 2016 White House races, but they are proceeding with more caution this election cycle.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: West Point cadet, 21, found dead in Florida during spring break trip
item.description: A West Point cadet was found dead this week following what officials believe to be an accidental drowning, the United States Military Academy has confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US Census changes how it categorizes people by race and ethnicity
item.description: Story at a glance The 2030 U.S. Census will include new race and ethnicity checkboxes for Hispanic people and people of Middle Eastern and North African descent, the Office of Management and Budget announced Thursday, a major change in how the government tracks demographics.
item.dc:creator: Nick Robertson, The Hill

item.title: Universal's 'How To Train Your Dragon' land revealed for Epic Universe
item.description: How to Train Your Dragon – Isle of Berk will be one of five worlds that will be featured in the all-new Universal Epic Universe set to open in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, Fox 5

item.title: Don't touch! These fuzzy bugs are taking over Florida
item.description: Springtime ushers in an influx of an infamous Florida pest. They appear to be everywhere, but be careful not to touch them.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, The Hill

item.title: King Charles III in Easter message calls for kindness after cancer diagnosis
item.description: King Charles III shared a message Thursday calling for kindness and friendship “in a time of need,” after he and Kate, the Princess of Wales, were both diagnosed with cancer.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Hidden camera found in staff bathroom at children's hospital in Rhode Island: police
item.description: The employee accused of placing the camera in the bathroom has since been fired, according to a hospital spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Doiron, Fox 5

item.title: Army Corps of Engineers to deploy 1,100 personnel to Baltimore after bridge collapse
item.description: The crumbling bridge sent multiple cars and construction workers who had been filling potholes plunging into the chilly waters below.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Disney, DeSantis-backed board reach settlement agreement
item.description: Disney and the state of Florida have reached a settlement agreement in their lawsuit about who controls Walt Disney World’s governing district.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: Hardy souls across New England and New York dig out after major winter storm
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Thousands of hardy souls across New England spent Sunday digging out after a major weekend storm dumped more than two feet of snow in some areas, caused multiple road accidents, downed power lines and left hundreds of thousands across the Northeast in the dark, some perhaps for days.
item.dc:creator: STEVE LeBLANC, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 85 years after a racist mob drove Opal Lee's family away, she's getting a new home on the same spot
item.description: FORT WORTH, Texas (AP) — When Opal Lee was 12, a racist mob drove her family out of their Texas home. Now, the 97-year-old community activist is getting closer to moving into a brand new home on the very same tree-lined corner lot in Fort Worth.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, WGN 9
